info.title: Detent API
info.description: Rate Limiter as a Service — the public /v1 surface. POST /v1/limit always returns HTTP 200; the `allowed` field carries the verdict (DECISIONS.md §2.2). Authenticate with an `dt_live_`/`dt_test_` API key as a bearer token. The /metrics endpoint serves Prometheus text and is not modeled here.

409.description: No billing customer yet — subscribe first

post.summary:
`POST /dashboard/billing/reconcile` — fast-path subscription sync on the
checkout success redirect. Retrieves the Checkout Session, verifies it
belongs to the caller, and — only if it was `paid` — fills in the account's
subscription status. Idempotent and safe alongside the webhook: it sets a
status only when none is set yet, so it never regresses a later
past_due/canceled the webhook may have written, and doesn't stamp
`last_stripe_event_at` (so subsequent webhook events still apply in order).

403.description: Session does not belong to this account

get.summary:
`GET /dashboard/billing/usage` — the session account's month rollup, for the
billing quota-to-upgrade nudge. 404s when billing is disabled (consistent
with the other `/dashboard/billing/*` routes); gated on the flag only, so it
needs no Stripe client.

`GET /dashboard/keys` — list the account's keys (active and revoked),
newest first. Never returns the raw key or its hash.

post.summary: `POST /dashboard/keys` — mint a new live API key for the account.

201.description: Key created; the raw secret is shown once

403.description: Active key limit reached for plan

delete.summary:
`DELETE /dashboard/keys/{id}` — soft-revoke the account's key (sets
`revoked_at`). Idempotent for an owned key; 404 if unknown or owned by
another account (no cross-tenant existence leak).

204.description: Key revoked (idempotent for an owned key)

get.summary: `GET /dashboard/namespaces` — the session account's namespace names, ascending.

delete.summary:
`DELETE /dashboard/namespaces/{ns}` — remove a namespace and its history for
the session account. Idempotent: returns 204 whether or not it existed (a
concurrent usage flush may already have removed or recreated it, and the SPA
reloads the list afterward). Purges the default rule and `usage_daily`
history; leaves `usage_monthly` (the per-account billing meter) and Redis TTL
counters untouched. See
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-dashboard-namespace-delete-design.md.

put.summary:
`PUT /dashboard/namespaces/{ns}/rules` — create or replace the namespace's
default rule. Idempotent; also materializes the namespace. Validation mirrors
`rules::create_rule`, plus a `concurrent_limit` guard (it is lease-based, not
a `/v1/limit` default — see DECISIONS.md §2.3 / §4.3).

403.description: Algorithm not available on the account's plan

get.summary: `GET /dashboard/namespaces/{ns}/stats` — aggregated usage for the session account.

post.description:
Step-up gated. Verifies the WebAuthn registration, CAS-claims the ceremony,
stores the credential with the given name (or a User-Agent-derived fallback),
emails a security notice, and returns the new row.

400.description: Bad ceremony state or name too long

post.description:
Step-up gated (`SudoAuth`). Excludes the account's existing credentials so a
second passkey can't be minted on an authenticator already enrolled.

delete.description:
Refused (409) when this would be the last passkey AND no unused recovery codes exist,
to prevent an account from locking itself out of every factor-2 option.

204.description: Passkey removed (idempotent for an owned id)

409.description: Refused: would leave the account with no second factor

patch.description:
Cosmetic, so access token only (no step-up). Name is trimmed and must be
1–64 characters. Returns the updated row, or 404 if the id is not this
account's.

400.description: Name empty or longer than 64 chars

404.description: No such passkey for this account

get.summary:
`GET /dashboard/rules` — every namespace for the session account with its
optional default rule, ascending. One query (LEFT JOIN), no N+1.

200.description: The account's namespaces + optional default rules
Lease decision — always 200; `allowed` carries the verdict, `lease_id` set when allowed

403.description: concurrent_limit is not available on the caller's plan (§4.3)

429.description: Monthly hard ceiling exceeded (§4.2 anti-abuse cap)

delete.summary:
`DELETE /v1/leases/{lease_id}` — release a lease. Idempotent. Unlike acquire,
a Redis outage is a `503` (NOT fail-open): a false "released" would mislead.

post.summary:
`POST /v1/limit` handler. `AuthContext` is injected by the auth middleware
(`crate::auth::require_auth`); the tenant `account_id` comes from the
validated key, never the request body (`DECISIONS.md §3.3`).

403.description: Algorithm not available on the caller's plan (§4.3)

429.description: Monthly hard ceiling exceeded (§4.2 anti-abuse cap)

delete.summary:
`DELETE /v1/namespaces/{ns}/keys/{key}` — reset the limiter counter for one
key. 204 (idempotent) on success; 503 if Redis is unavailable (NOT
fail-open — a silent success while Redis is down would mislead the operator).

get.summary: `GET /v1/namespaces/{ns}/rules` — fetch the namespace's rule (404 if none).

post.summary: `POST /v1/namespaces/{ns}/rules` — create or replace the namespace's rule.

403.description: Algorithm not available on the caller's plan (§4.3)

get.summary: `GET /v1/webhook` — current config (404 if none). The secret is not returned.

put.summary: `PUT /v1/webhook` — set or replace the account's webhook (rotates the secret).

403.description: Webhooks require the Pro or Scale plan

has_billing_customer.description:
True once a Stripe customer exists — the SPA routes plan changes to the
Billing Portal (existing customer) vs. Checkout (new).

trial_ends_at.description: RFC3339; null while no trial / already subscribed past trial.

AcquireBody.description:
Body for `POST /v1/leases`. `limit`/`window_ms` are optional: inline wins,
else the namespace's `concurrent_limit` rule fills them (`§2.3`).

limit.description: Max concurrent slots. Optional when a concurrent rule exists.

window_ms.description: Lease TTL in milliseconds. Optional when a concurrent rule exists.

AcquireResponse.description:
Response for `POST /v1/leases`. Always HTTP 200 (`§2.2`); `allowed` carries
the verdict. `lease_id` is present only when `allowed` is true.

When `lease_id` is `null` due to fail-open (Redis unavailable), `active` and
`reset_ms` are not meaningful measurements — they reflect no real state.

active.description: Active lease count after this call.

reset_ms.description: When denied, ms until the soonest-expiring lease frees a slot.

name.description: Optional device name (1–64 chars). Empty/absent → derived from User-Agent.

BillingSessionUrl.description:
Response body for both billing session endpoints: the Stripe-hosted URL to
redirect the browser to.

key.description: The raw `dt_live_…` secret — shown ONCE, never retrievable again.

ErrorResponse.description: The JSON body returned for every error response: `{ "error": "<message>" }`.

error.description: Human-readable error message. Never contains internal/driver detail.

LimitBody.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/limit`. The wire format uses `window_ms`
(`DECISIONS.md §2.1`); `algorithm` deserializes from its snake_case name.

`algorithm`/`limit`/`window_ms` are optional: when omitted they fall back to
the namespace's default rule (`§2.3`). Inline values win per-field.

LimitResponse.description:
Response body. The endpoint always returns HTTP 200 (`DECISIONS.md §2.2`);
`allowed` carries the verdict.

MonthSummary.description:
Current-month rollup + soft-quota state (`§4.2`), per account (across all
namespaces). This is the data source for the dashboard quota banner.

NamespaceRule.description:
One namespace and its optional default rule. `rule` is null when the
namespace exists (e.g. seen by a `/v1/limit` call) but has no default yet.

active.description: Active lease count after this call.

released.description: Whether a held lease was removed (false if already expired/released).

name.description: New display name (1–64 characters after trimming).

Rule.description:
The default rule for a namespace. `limit`/`window_ms` are `u64` on the wire
but stored as `int`/`bigint`; values are range-checked before insert.

RuleBody.description: Body for `POST /v1/namespaces/{ns}/rules`. The namespace comes from the path.

api_key.description: Detent API key as a bearer token
